chunk_summary: Evaluation of a toddler with milk overuse and iron deficiency anemia, focusing on dietary assessment, feeding habits, and structured interventions to improve iron intake and reduce milk displacement.
learning_objectives: Understand the impact of excessive milk consumption on iron absorption and dietary balance in toddlers.
Learn to conduct a targeted dietary assessment, including 24-hour recall and iron-focused frequency screening.
Develop structured intervention plans to address iron deficiency anemia, including dietary changes and iron supplementation.
Recognize when to escalate evaluation for underlying conditions if anemia persists despite adherence.
key_facts: Excessive cow’s milk intake can displace iron-rich solids, contributing to iron deficiency anemia in toddlers.
Recommended milk volume should be limited, and solids should be offered before milk to improve dietary balance.
Iron-rich foods like pulses, eggs, and meat should be paired with vitamin C sources to enhance absorption.
Therapeutic iron supplementation should follow local guidelines, with adherence monitored for side effects like constipation.
Follow-up includes reassessment of diet and hemoglobin levels in 4–6 weeks, with further evaluation if anemia persists.
reference_quote: Prolonged bottle-feeding and excessive milk consumption are significant contributors to iron deficiency anemia in toddlers. (AAFP, 2006)
document_key_topics: Impact of milk overuse on iron deficiency
